sections.paragraph:
m.-

(1) dung, so called from its globular form, i.q. גֵּלֶל, see the root No. 1; 1Ki 14:10. Arab. حَلَّةُ globular dung of animals, as of camels, sheep.

(2) circumstance, cause, reason; Germ. Um=ftand; comp. as to the etymology סִבָּה, אֹודֹות, whence בִּגְלַל, with suffix בִּגְלָלֶךָ, בִּגְלַלְכֶם, prep. because of. Gen 39:5 Deu 15:10 18:12 Jer 11:17 15:4 . To this answers the Arabic من جلالك and اجللك من (with Elif prosthetic).

(3) [Galal], pr.n. of two men (perh. “weighty,” “worthy,” as أَجَلُّ)-(a) 1Ch 9:15.-(b) 1Ch 9:16 Neh 11:17.
